core_model.structure_summary: An iterative update that at each step minimizes a linearized objective plus a Bregman-divergence proximity term, equivalently a metric-preconditioned gradient step obtained by discretizing a continuous flow.
core_model.canonical_form:
x_{k+1} = \arg\min_{x} \; \eta \langle x, 
abla F(x_k)angle + D_\psi(x, x_k), \quad	ext{equivalently}\quad 
abla\psi(x_{k+1}) = 
abla\psi(x_k) - \eta 
abla F(x_k), \quad \dot x = -H(x)^{-1}
abla F(x)

variables.meaning: scalar function whose gradient drives the update
metric tensor (Hessian of a potential) preconditioning the gradient

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order continuous flow moving down the gradient of a scalar function preconditioned by the inverse of a state-dependent metric tensor.

notes: Core object is the discrete mirror-descent update; the paper's central claim is that it is a 'partial' discretization of the Riemannian gradient flow xdot = -H^{-1} grad F, which is the skeleton. Two faces of one structure (ode <-> discrete_update).
